task: Write a Python function collapse_delims(s) that splits the string on commas, treats any run of consecutive commas as one split, drops empty pieces at the start and end, and returns the pieces rejoined with a single comma between each. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: The provided code meets all the specified requirements.
trace:
To achieve the required functionality, we first replace consecutive commas with a single comma using the `replace()` method. Then, we split the string on commas and remove any empty strings at the beginning and end of the resulting list. Finally, we join the remaining pieces back together into a single string, with each piece separated by a comma.

An edge case that's handled is when there are multiple consecutive empty strings at the start or end of the input string. We remove all such empty strings from the beginning and end of the list, ensuring that they don't affect the final output.
